moat_sources: Integrated hardware, software, services, and distribution ecosystem
High customer switching costs from device, account, and service integration
Global brand, developer ecosystem, and scale purchasing advantages
business_quality.pricing_power: strong in premium devices; more limited in price-sensitive international markets

key_drivers: Installed-base expansion and upgrade cycles for iPhone and other devices
Services monetization, including App Store, subscriptions, payments, and cloud offerings
Continued premiumization and cross-selling across the Apple ecosystem
key_risks: iPhone concentration and cyclicality in global consumer-device demand
Regulatory pressure on App Store economics and default-distribution agreements
Supply-chain concentration and geopolitical exposure, particularly in Greater China
stage_2_conclusion: Apple fits Type B because it pairs mature-scale growth with exceptional profitability, recurring ecosystem economics, and substantial free-cash-flow generation. The main diligence focus is whether services growth and the installed base can offset hardware-cycle and regulatory pressure.
